item.title: Presentations with reveal.js
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya / @rmarianoa
item.description:
Old-ôÙfashôÙioned PPôÙT's preôÙsenôÙtaôÙtions are from the 90's, and let's face it, we are in the age of the web browsôÙer. So, the last time I had to gave a talk, I deôÙcidôÙed to use a betôÙter techôÙniôÙcal supôÙport.

AfôÙter a quick search for alôÙterôÙnaôÙtives, I found many opôÙtionôÙs, inôÙcludôÙing well-ôÙknown liôÙbraries, unôÙtil I fiôÙnalôÙly deôÙcidôÙed for reôÙveal.js.

It is writôÙten in JavaScript with good CSS themes, and it does not reôÙquire exôÙpert knowlôÙedge on those techôÙnoloôÙgies. In orôÙder to play the preôÙsenôÙtaôÙtion, you launch an HTML file from a web browsôÙer or yôÙou can alôÙso run it with a statôÙic servôÙer.

AdôÙvanôÙtages:

  • VerôÙsion conôÙtrol: GivôÙen the fact that your preôÙsenôÙtaôÙtion is made from source code, it is posôÙsiôÙble ôÙto track changes by usôÙing git.
  • A betôÙter cross-ôÙplatôÙform supôÙport: it does not reôÙly on a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar softôÙware in a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar verôÙsion to be present (web browsers are ubiqôÙuiôÙtous nowaôÙdays).
  • ComôÙpatôÙiôÙbilôÙiôÙty: WYSIôÙWYG.
  • Able to host your preôÙsenôÙtaôÙtion in the cloud and acôÙcess it from anyôÙwhere.

ReaôÙsons to use it / nice things about it:

  • We are in 2014
  • SupôÙports MarkôÙdown lanôÙguage

To be clear: I am not sayôÙing that this is a betôÙter alôÙterôÙnaôÙtive beôÙcause is newôÙer of modôÙern, but beôÙcause of the adôÙvanôÙtages listôÙed. In othôÙer wordôÙs, if we count with deôÙvelôÙoped toolôÙs at our disôÙposôÙal, it would be a good idea to use them.

Here are some simôÙple and baôÙsic exôÙamôÙples of preôÙsenôÙtaôÙtions I am workôÙing on. (DISôÙCLAIMER: they might be in difôÙferôÙent lanôÙguages, and the page is in proôÙgress).

item.title: Starting a gnome-shell extension
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Since I moved from UbunôÙtu to FeôÙdoôÙra earôÙly this year, I changed my deskôÙtop enôÙviôÙronôÙment from uniôÙty to Gnome ShelôÙl.

I must say it is a great exôÙpeôÙriôÙence. At the beôÙginôÙning I was thinkôÙing on what was betôÙter comôÙpared to uniôÙty (beôÙcause I did not like UniôÙty). ReôÙgardôÙing this ôÙcomôÙparôÙiôÙson I found a more staôÙble and usôÙable winôÙdow manôÙagôÙer. I still preôÙfer someôÙthing simôÙpler perôÙhapôÙs, but it works good and it is nice.

Just out of cuôÙriosôÙiôÙty, one day I startôÙed manôÙagôÙing some exôÙtenôÙsionôÙs. Oh, by the way, yôÙou will probôÙaôÙbly be inôÙstalling some exôÙtenôÙsions for Gnome shelôÙl, some of them are useôÙful and some othôÙers make it more usôÙable. That drove me to the exôÙtenôÙsions deôÙvelôÙopôÙer API, which turned out to be quite simôÙple and inôÙterôÙestôÙing.

HavôÙing played a litôÙtle bit, I deôÙcidôÙed to code a (very) simôÙple exôÙtenôÙsion that does just one sôÙinôÙgle thing. I called it "simôÙple-ôÙname" and it is availôÙable at: SimôÙple-ôÙName-ExôÙtenôÙsion

It reôÙmemôÙbers some litôÙtle deôÙtail I was missôÙing from some preôÙviôÙous winôÙdow manôÙagers that I have used along the years, which is to disôÙplay the userôÙname of the curôÙrentôÙly logged-in usôÙer.

I must say that, I code it just for the sake of learnôÙing and exôÙperôÙiôÙmentôÙing with and API and a bit of JavaScripôÙt. In adôÙdiôÙtion I found very inôÙterôÙestôÙing to see fronôÙt-end reôÙlatôÙed techôÙnoloôÙgies like JavaScript or CSS used in the deskôÙtop enôÙviôÙronôÙment (not that is someôÙthing new, but still funôÙny).

To sum up, I wantôÙed to highôÙlight that is inôÙterôÙestôÙing to learn new techôÙnoloôÙgies, even with litôÙtle and simôÙple steps like a new API, liôÙbrary, etc. It is not for the techôÙnolôÙoôÙgy itôÙself, but for the sake of learnôÙing :)
